key_points: Water is essential for life, ecosystems, agriculture, and industry; it is unevenly distributed globally
The water cycle involves complex processes like transpiration, infiltration, runoff, and percolation
Groundwater depletion due to overuse and contamination is a major concern
Water pollution includes chemical, biological, and thermal pollution; affects humans and aquatic organisms
Methods like desalination, artificial recharge, and sustainable water management help address water scarcity
examples: Desalination plants convert seawater to freshwater in arid regions
Excess extraction of groundwater in Punjab and Rajasthan has caused lowering of water table
Eutrophication of lakes due to nutrient pollution from fertilizers harms aquatic life
Rainwater harvesting and check dams restore groundwater in drought-prone areas
Industrial chemical spills and untreated sewage pollute rivers and affect human health
formulas: Water cycle = Evaporation + Transpiration + Condensation + Precipitation + Infiltration + Runoff
Groundwater table depth = Initial water table – Extraction + Recharge
exam_tips: Understand advanced stages of the water cycle including percolation and transpiration
Learn human impact on water resources, including depletion and pollution
Include real-life examples of water management and conservation
Explain causes and effects of water scarcity with diagrams or case studies
